visual_anchor: Terminal window as the central trust object with layered security context.
layout_rules: Place the terminal window as the dominant visual anchor on the right side.
Use the left side for trust claims and governance labels, not dense text.
Layer subtle security indicators around the terminal without cluttering it.
Maintain clear separation between the terminal object and explanatory text.
alignment_rules: Terminal window must sit within a consistent right-side frame with controlled padding.
Left-side trust claims align to a shared vertical grid.
Security indicator layers must not overlap with terminal content area.
Governance labels align to the left edge of the terminal frame.

image_usage.placement_guidance: Use a terminal window SVG or screenshot as the right-side hero object. The terminal should show realistic command output or code that reinforces trust.

highlight_grammar: Use accent color for security indicators and trust badges.
Keep terminal content in monospace with syntax highlighting if applicable.
Use neutral colors for governance labels to avoid visual competition.
Apply subtle glow or border emphasis to the terminal frame only.
anti_patterns: Terminal window too small to be the visual anchor.
Dense text competing with the terminal for attention.
Multiple competing visual elements around the terminal.
Security indicators that obscure terminal content.
reuse_notes: Use this pattern when explaining security architecture or trust boundaries.
The terminal content should be realistic and relevant to the trust claim.
Adapt the terminal styling to match the overall theme while maintaining recognizability.
